  Manages a free Let's Encrypt certificate on shared hosting (installLetsEncrypt/removeCertificate on /vh/ssl). Identified by the domain it covers; the issuance inputs force replacement, while autoprolong toggles in place. Issuance is asynchronous — Create waits for the certificate to appear.

# sweb_letsencrypt (Resource)

Manages a free Let's Encrypt certificate on shared hosting (installLetsEncrypt/removeCertificate on /vh/ssl). Identified by the `domain` it covers; the issuance inputs force replacement, while `autoprolong` toggles in place. Issuance is asynchronous — Create waits for the certificate to appear.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Issue a free Let's Encrypt certificate for a domain on shared hosting.
# Identified by the domain it covers; issuance is asynchronous (Create waits).
resource "sweb_letsencrypt" "site" {
  domain      = "example.com"
  autoprolong = true # renew automatically before expiry (updates in place)
  # wildcard  = true          # request a wildcard cert (forces replacement)
  # virtdom   = "sub.example.com"
  # challenge = "acme"        # "acme" | "dns"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `domain` (String) The fully-qualified domain the certificate covers (its identity).

### Optional

- `autoprolong` (Boolean) Whether auto-prolongation is enabled. Toggled in place via editAutoprolong.
- `challenge` (String) Validation type: "acme" or "dns". Set at issuance (forces replacement).
- `ip` (String) Target IP, or "sni" for SNI. Set at issuance (forces replacement).
- `timeouts` (Block, Optional) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--timeouts))
- `virtdom` (String) Subdomain to cover, e.g. "sub.example.com". Set at issuance (forces replacement).
- `wildcard` (Boolean) Request a wildcard certificate. Set at issuance (forces replacement).

### Read-Only

- `certificate_id` (Number) The API's numeric certificate id (the removeCertificate/editAutoprolong key).
- `id` (String) Resource id (equal to domain).
- `status` (String) The certificate status reported by the API.
- `valid_to` (String) The certificate expiry date reported by the API.

<a id="nestedblock--timeouts"></a>
### Nested Schema for `timeouts`

Optional:

- `create` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import an existing Let's Encrypt certificate by the domain it covers.
# The issuance inputs (wildcard/virtdom/ip/challenge) aren't recoverable from the
# API, so set them in your HCL afterwards.
terraform import sweb_letsencrypt.site example.com
```
